ListItem.Para: Left and center pitot probes are inspected before each departure.
Left and center pitot probe heater systems operate normally.
Left and center pitot air data modules operate normally.
Static air data modules operate normally.
AIR DATA/ATT instrument source switches operate normally.
Approach minimums do not require its use.

Note.Para: The left, center and right pitot probes are interchangeable. Refer to the Illustrated Parts Database (IPD) for location and part number compatibility.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Inspect the left and center pitot probes prior to each departure (AMM DDG 30-31-01).

ListItem.Para: Visually examine the left and center pitot probe external installations for damage and unwanted material in the pitot probe opening. If damage or unwanted material is noted, perform pitot probe - inspection.

OperationNote.Para: The NO LAND 3 advisory message will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: Right and center pitot probes are inspected before each departure.
Right and center pitot probe heater systems operate normally.
Right and center pitot air data modules operate normally.
Static air data modules operate normally.
AIR DATA/ATT instrument source switches operate normally.
Approach minimums do not require its use.

Note.Para: The left, center and right pitot probes are interchangeable.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Inspect the right and center pitot probes before each departure (AMM DDG 30-31-01).

ListItem.Para: Visually examine the right and center pitot probe external installations for damage and unwanted material in the pitot probe opening. If damage or unwanted material is noted, perform pitot probe - inspection.
Maximum landing capability is CAT IIIA.
The NO LAND 3 advisory message will be displayed.
Left and right pitot probes are inspected before each departure.
Left and right pitot probe heater systems operate normally.
Left and right pitot air data modules operate normally.
Static air data modules operate normally.
AIR DATA/ATT instrument source switches operate normally.
Airplane is not operated in visible moisture.
Airplane is not operated in known or forecast icing conditions.
Approach minimums do not require its use.
Flight remains within 120 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.

Note.Para: The left, center and right pitot probes are interchangeable. Refer to the Illustrated Parts Database (IPD) for location and part number compatibility.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Inspect the left and right pitot probes before each departure (AMM DDG 30-31-01).

ListItem.Para: Visually examine the left and right pitot probe external installations for damage and unwanted material in the pitot probe opening. If damage or unwanted material is noted, perform pitot probe - inspection.

OperationNote.Para: The NO LAND 3 advisory message will be displayed.
